description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, tilted photovoltaic panel array mounted on a robust, geometric base. The base is composed of a series of angular, stepped, and block-like structures, likely designed to provide stability and house electrical components. The solar panel itself is composed of multiple rectangular cells arranged in a grid, supported by a visible frame. Adjacent to the panel are several raised modules and enclosures, which may contain inverters, junction boxes, or other electrical hardware. The surface of the base displays a patterned texture, possibly for drainage or anti-slip purposes. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as parks, rooftops, or utility areas.
description: A large, tilted solar panel array mounted on a patterned, blocky base with several raised modules and enclosures, suitable for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: The tilted solar panel dominates the view, with the patterned base and several raised modules visible behind and to the right.
description_view_1: The solar panel is seen edge-on, revealing the full width of the base and the arrangement of blocky structures and enclosures.
description_view_2: The panel is viewed from the opposite side, showing the angular support structure and more of the patterned base surface.
description_view_3: The view is from behind the panel, highlighting the back of the base, the support structure, and the absence of visible cabling.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a geometric, likely concrete and metal support structure
attribute_array_configuration: single tilted panel composed of multiple rectangular cells in a grid arrangement
